_meta.description: WashU course equivalency table. Maps non-standard codes (CSE-E81, old CHEM codes, etc.) to their official WashU equivalents. Used by the degree audit to match student courses against requirements.

CSE-E81 131.title: Introduction to Computer Science
CSE-E81 131.description: Principles of computer science. CSE-E81 131 and CSE 131 are identical courses.
CSE-E81 131.source: WashU Bulletin CSE 131 course description: 'Same as CSE 131. Students may not receive credit for both.'

CSE-E81 247.title: Microcontrollers and Embedded Systems
CSE-E81 247.description: Embedded systems programming in C. CSE-E81 247 and CSE 2407 are identical courses.
CSE-E81 247.source: WashU Bulletin CSE 2407 course description: 'Same as CSE-E81 247. Students may not receive credit for both.'

CSE-E81 132.title: Introduction to Computer Engineering
CSE-E81 132.description: Topics include transistor, digital logic, and computer architecture. NOT equivalent to CSE 1302.
CSE-E81 132.note: CSE 1302 is Object-Oriented Software Development (C++/Java). CSE-E81 132 is circuits/digital design. They cover completely different material.

SDS 3211.title: Statistics for Data Science I
SDS 3211.note: E10's actual course — already using official code

CHEM 111A.title: First-Year Chemistry
CHEM 111A.note: CHEM 111 or CHEM 151 or CHEM 161 satisfies the Chemistry foundation for Biology majors

CHEM 151.title: Principles of Chemistry I
CHEM 151.note: Already official code — satisfies CHEM 151 requirement

PHYSICS 191.title: Physics I
PHYSICS 191.note: PHYSICS 197 and PHYSICS 198 satisfy the Physics requirement for Biology majors. PHYSICS 191 is the alternate code for the same course.

MATH 309.title: Matrix Algebra
MATH 309.note: MATH 309 is the old code for Matrix Algebra. The Biology major accepts MATH 1510, MATH 2200, or MATH 3200. E10's MATH 309 = MATH 2200.

BIOL 500A.title: Independent Research
BIOL 500A.note: Research credit — does not satisfy any specific course requirement, but may count toward upper-level biology electives if graded A-F

_rules.description: How to apply these equivalencies in the audit
matching_rules: When checking if a requirement is satisfied, look up the student's actual course code in this table
If equiv_status is 'confirmed', the course satisfies the requirement for the official code
If equiv_status is 'not_equivalent', the course does NOT satisfy that requirement even if the codes look similar
If equiv_status is 'official_code', the course already uses the correct code
If equiv_status is 'lab', check if the corresponding lecture course is also taken
If equiv_status is 'research', it may count toward elective requirements but not core requirements
